position: Start of response line (first element of the reply)
explanation: Three-digit numeric reply code used by SMTP clients to determine next action; the full code identifies the specific response type and MUST be used by automata instead of reply text.

position: First digit of the three-digit reply code
explanation: Severity/class of the reply as encoded by the first digit of the reply code (used to distinguish success, intermediate, transient failure, permanent failure).

position: Second digit of the three-digit reply code
explanation: Category of the reply encoded by the second digit (used to indicate syntax, information, connections, or mail-system related categories).

position: Third digit of the three-digit reply code
explanation: Finer-grained detail of the reply encoded by the third digit (reserved for the finest gradation of information within the category).
